question.text: What is the total premium outflow for buying 10 lots of NIFTY 25,000 call options at Rs 300 per unit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Premium per lot = Premium per unit × Lot size = 300 × 25 = Rs 7,500. Total for 10 lots = 7,500 × 10 = Rs 75,000. This is the maximum loss (premium paid) for the option buyer. **The Trap:** Rs 3,000 uses premium × 10 without lot size. Rs 30,000 uses 100 per lot. Rs 7,500 is for 1 lot only. **Cross-Exam Context:** Premium calculation with lot sizes is fundamental across all option markets. NIFTY lot = 25. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rs 75,000 is both the maximum loss AND the margin required for this long option position. Compare this with the margin for 10 NIFTY futures lots (typically Rs 15-20 lakhs) — options require much less capital.
